extra.reasoning:
The property G(opted_out implies not sent) is violated by transition t_resume_glitch.

Initial state: opted_out=false, sent=false, ticks=0.

Trace:
1. optout (t_optout): opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=0.
2-7. tick x6 (t_tick): each increments ticks (capped at 6) and sets sent=false. After 6 ticks: opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=6.
8. send_attempt: with opted_out=true and ticks>=6, transition t_resume_glitch [on send_attempt if (opted_out and ticks >= 6): sent := true] is enabled and fires, producing opted_out=true, sent=true.

In this reachable state opted_out=true AND sent=true, which falsifies "opted_out implies not sent". The trace is 8 events, within the 10-event bound.

Note on rule interaction: on send_attempt with opted_out=true and ticks>=6, both t_send_blocked (guard opted_out) and t_resume_glitch (guard opted_out and ticks>=6) are enabled. No priority is specified, so t_resume_glitch is a legitimate enabled choice. Since the property must hold on EVERY reachable execution, the mere existence of this enabled glitch transition that reaches a violating state breaks the safety property. (Were ticks < 6, only t_send_blocked applies and sent stays false; the bug requires ticks to be saturated at 6 first.)
